---
title: "Teaching"
toc: false
---

I enjoy teaching mathematics through extensive problem solving and visual intuition. My teaching experience includes coaching students in preparation for national mathematics competitions in Russia, and serving as a teaching assistant for an undergraduate optimization course.

## Reading Groups

- **Applications of Linear Algebra**  
  *Directed Reading Group (DRP 26) --- Rice University, Spring 2025*  
  Organized a directed reading group on applications of linear algebra, with emphasis geometry, algorithms, and optimization. The group centered on selected readings from:
  - J. Matoušek, B. Gärtner *"Understanding and Using Linear Programming"*
  - J. Matoušek *"Thirty-three Miniatures: Mathematical and Algorithmic Applications of Linear Algebra"*,

  along with short papers on the role of determinants in linear algebra. [Schedule and readings](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1J_3LoaARyf10piFmOvUceF6997kdvSCfeQN4c0UNBRU/edit?usp=sharing){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}

## Teaching Experience

- **Tapia Camps at Rice University**  
  *Mathematics Instructor --- Summer 2025*  
  Taught [Techniques of a Pro Mathematician](https://tapiacenter.rice.edu/techniques-pro-mathematician){target="_blank" rel="noopener"}, a week-long course for high-school students on mathematical problem solving, proof strategies, and the core principles to learn and do mathematics more efficiently and effectively.

- **CMOR 360: Introduction to Optimization and Operations Research**  
  *Teaching Assistant, Rice University — Spring 2025*  
  Held recitation sessions and office hours, created and graded assignments, and helped students with course material in optimization and operations research.

- **Lyceum “The Second School”**  
  *Competitive Mathematics Coach — September 2021 to May 2022*  
  Led two weekly two-hour classes for students preparing for the Russian National Mathematics Olympiad and other regional mathematics competitions.
